title: RepoBrief-Familienpublikation generationskohärent machen
goal: Make publication of multiple stable RepoBrief family manifests recoverable and generation-coherent so readers cannot observe an unintended mixture of old and new family generations after interruption or concurrent publication.

acceptance.assertion: All family manifests from one publication are bound to one explicit generation identity and readers have one documented rule for selecting a complete committed generation.
Injected failures before, between and after family publication cannot leave an unintended mixed generation as the authoritative read state; recovery is deterministic and tested.
Concurrent publishers are serialized or reconciled with a fail-closed protocol whose winner and rollback behavior are testable.
Existing single-family consumers continue to work or receive an explicit versioned migration path; consumer path validation remains fail-closed.
Focused crash/concurrency tests, the complete Lenskit suite, maintainability ratchets and real CLI dogfood pass on the exact implementation head.
The result does not claim distributed consensus, cross-host transactional filesystems, remote freshness or semantic truth.
